deep_dive.title: Interpretable and Verifiable Hardware Generation with LLM-Driven Stepwise Refinement
deep_dive.abstract: Large language models (LLMs) have achieved remarkable success in software development. However, they are susceptible to hallucinations, meaning that they can introduce subtle semantic and logical errors. Due to the high stakes in chip design and manufacturing, hardware engineers are still reluctant to rely on LLMs for register-transfer level (RTL) generation. In this paper, we propose a hardware generation framework that combines the creativity and broad knowledge of LLMs with the explainability and mathematical rigor of formal methods. Specifically, we devise a set of transformation rules that cover various design decisions and hardware features. By iteratively applying these rules, an LLM agent can convert a design specification into an RTL program with guaranteed correctness. Experimental results demonstrate the effectiveness and efficiency of the framework.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: LLM-driven hardware generation with formal stepwise refinement
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 结合形式化逐步精化的LLM硬件生成
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 结合形式化逐步精化的LLM硬件生成
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 结合形式化逐步精化的LLM硬件生成
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: A hardware generation framework combining LLM creativity with formal stepwise refinement rules for verifiable RTL designs.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: LLM在硬件生成中存在幻觉问题。本文提出将LLM与形式化方法结合的框架，通过一组转换规则逐步将设计规范精化为RTL。该方法结合了LLM的创造力和形式化方法的可解释性与数学严谨性，可生成可验证的硬件。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: LLM在硬件生成中存在幻觉问题。本文提出将LLM与形式化方法结合的框架，通过一组转换规则逐步将设计规范精化为RTL。该方法结合了LLM的创造力和形式化方法的可解释性与数学严谨性，可生成可验证的硬件。
deep_dive.matched_query_text: hardware formal verification using large language models

quick_skim.title: Shift-Left High-Level Synthesis Verification via Knowledge-Augmented LLM Agent
quick_skim.abstract: High-Level Synthesis (HLS) relies on transforming original C specifications into synthesizable HLS-oriented C (HLS-C) implementations. Functional consistency verification between original C specifications and HLS-C implementations is a critical yet labor-intensive task in HLS design flows. While Large Language Models (LLMs) have recently shown promise in automated testbench generation, their stochastic nature often leads to insufficient coverage, inconsistent verification environments, and unreliable equivalence checking results. To address these limitations, we propose a knowledge-augmented, agent-driven shift-left verification framework for automated functional consistency checking between original C and HLS-C implementations before synthesis. The framework introduces a Dual-Tier Consistency Checking mechanism that jointly enforces static structural alignment and dynamic behavioral equivalence between paired testbenches, while integrating symbolic execution and coverage-driven refinement to improve verification completeness. Furthermore, we construct a heterogeneous HLS Verification Knowledge Graph to provide topology-aware reasoning priors for testbench generation, and design an autonomous verification agent to orchestrate iterative refinement and failure diagnosis across heterogeneous toolchains. Experimental results on 107 HLS benchmark pairs demonstrate that the proposed framework achieves 0.9826 average coverage and 0.9533 dynamic consistency, outperforming representative AST-based, retrieval-augmented, and iterative agent-based baselines. https://github.com/cz-5f/HLS-LeVeri.git
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: LLM agent for HLS functional consistency verification
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 基于知识增强LLM代理的HLS功能一致性验证
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 基于知识增强LLM代理的HLS功能一致性验证
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 基于知识增强LLM代理的HLS功能一致性验证
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Knowledge-augmented LLM agent for shift-left verification of C-to-HLS-C functional consistency before synthesis.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 高级综合中，C规范与HLS-C实现的功能一致性验证劳动密集。本文提出知识增强的LLM代理驱动的左移验证框架，在综合前自动检查一致性。该方法克服了LLM随机性导致的覆盖不足和环境不一致问题。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 高级综合中，C规范与HLS-C实现的功能一致性验证劳动密集。本文提出知识增强的LLM代理驱动的左移验证框架，在综合前自动检查一致性。该方法克服了LLM随机性导致的覆盖不足和环境不一致问题。

quick_skim.title: Structural Preservation and the Logical Expressiveness of Graph Neural Networks
quick_skim.abstract: Bridges between graph neural networks (GNNs) and logical formalisms have been established by fixing architectural choices, such as the types of aggregation, combination, and activation functions. These choices define restricted classes of GNNs for which tight correspondences with logical formalisms can be obtained, by showing that logical formulae can be translated into equivalent GNNs and, conversely, that GNNs can be translated into equivalent formulae.   In this paper we take a semantic perspective by establishing the logical expressiveness of classes of GNN classifiers that are preserved under structural properties: embeddings (extensions), injective homomorphisms, and homomorphisms. We show that, for each such property, there exists a fragment of graded modal logic characterising the class of GNNs. In particular, preservation under embeddings, injective homomorphisms, and homomorphisms corresponds to existential graded modal logic, its existential-positive fragment, and existential-positive modal logic, respectively. These results characterise the expressiveness of broad classes of GNNs independently of specific architectural choices, but we also show that each of these classes admits a GNN architecture of the same expressiveness.   Technically, our approach uses a new well-quasi-order result for trees of bounded height, yielding finite representations of unravelling-invariant classes.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: Theoretical analysis of GNN logical expressiveness, relevant to GNN-based verification tool selection
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: GNN逻辑表达能力的理论分析，与基于GNN的验证工具选择相关
quick_skim.llm_evidence: GNN逻辑表达能力的理论分析，与基于GNN的验证工具选择相关
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: GNN逻辑表达能力的理论分析，与基于GNN的验证工具选择相关
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Establishes the logical expressiveness of GNN classifiers under structural preservation properties, bridging GNNs and logical formalisms for potential applications in verification.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文从语义角度研究图神经网络（GNN）的逻辑表达能力，分析了在保持嵌入、单同态和同态等结构性质下GNN分类器的表达能力。结果表明不同结构保持性质对应不同逻辑片段，为GNN在形式验证中的应用提供了理论依据，尤其是等价性和工具选择等场景。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 该论文从语义角度研究图神经网络（GNN）的逻辑表达能力，分析了在保持嵌入、单同态和同态等结构性质下GNN分类器的表达能力。结果表明不同结构保持性质对应不同逻辑片段，为GNN在形式验证中的应用提供了理论依据，尤其是等价性和工具选择等场景。
quick_skim.matched_query_text: graph neural network for equivalence checking and verification tool selection
